scope.projectRules: AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md are loaded through PlatformRuntime as prioritized project.instructions sections without mutating the user prompt.
scope.outputContracts: json-object, json-file, file, and command-plan contracts are represented in platform contracts, prompt assembly, model JSON mode, final verification, and repair failure handling.
scope.verificationExpectations: schema, artifact, check-command, and generated-output expectations run through the normal final verifier path with bounded diagnostics.
scope.executionGovernance: shell, git, test, and package process tools use noninteractive execution profiles, pager/editor avoidance, and bounded output evidence.
scope.memoryContext: current context, PageIndex, lossless context, permanent memory, provider cache, and external memory hooks are separated in diagnostics and prompt priority.
scope.scoring: delivery capability scoring keeps fake/replay evidence separate; fake, replay-only, missing, partial, planned, absent, and unavailable states do not receive live delivery credit.
scope.adapterBoundary: Terminal-Bench adapter remains a local thin bridge: it calls the CLI command-plan output contract, executes returned commands in the harness, records logs, and does not encode task-specific SQLite or artifact semantics.

liveDeliveryEvidence.reason: This change systemizes product layers and verifier contracts; the existing live evidence remains the source of live delivery scoring and replay cache. Replay and fake evidence are not counted as live credit.
